engineGuide.h2: Mercedes Benz CLA Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mercedes Benz CLA across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.1 Litre Diesel
entries.history: The OM651 was Mercedes' high-volume four-cylinder diesel throughout the 2010s, powering the C117 CLA with proven torque and economy.
compatibleVariants: CLA 200d, CLA 200d Shooting Brake, CLA 220 CDI, CLA 220 CDI Shooting Brake, CLA 220d (early production)
commonFailures: Head gasket failure between cylinders 2 and 3 - Mercedes issued a service campaign for this defect
AdBlue dosing valve crystallisation and clogging (Euro 6 models)
Fuel injector seal leaks causing misfires and diesel contamination
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM651 Mercedes Benz CLA engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0 Litre Diesel
entries.history: The N47D20C is a BMW-sourced engine fitted to CLA 220d models during the C117 facelift period - notorious in the UK for timing chain failures that often write off otherwise healthy cars.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure causing chain stretch and rattle on cold start (typically 60,000-100,000 miles)
EGR cooler failure leading to coolant loss and potential engine overheating
Turbocharger bearing failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for N47D20C Mercedes Benz CLA engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0 Litre Diesel
entries.history: The OM654 replaced the OM651 as Mercedes' modular four-cylinder diesel, debuting in the C118 CLA with stepped bowl combustion and reduced internal friction.
compatibleVariants: CLA 200d, CLA 200d Shooting Brake, CLA 220d, CLA 220d 4MATIC, CLA 220d Shooting Brake
commonFailures: Oil leaks from the oil cooler, pressure sensor and sump area (appears after 100,000 km)
SCR catalytic converter thermal aging - motornear position leads to faster degradation
NOx sensor drift requiring replacement
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM654 Mercedes Benz CLA engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5 Litre Diesel
entries.history: The OM607 is a Renault-sourced 1.5 dCi engine used in entry-level CLA 180d models, offering strong economy at the cost of refinement.
commonFailures: Injector seal leakage causing diesel odour and misfiring
Turbocharger failure (variable vane mechanism seizes)
DPF clogging on short journeys
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM607 Mercedes Benz CLA engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The M270 was Mercedes' first transversely-mounted turbocharged four-cylinder petrol, featuring direct injection and CAMTRONIC variable valve lift across CLA 200 and CLA 250 models.
compatibleVariants: CLA 200, CLA 200 Shooting Brake, CLA 250, CLA 250 4MATIC, CLA 250 Shooting Brake
commonFailures: Crankshaft surface damage - factory defect prompting replacement campaign on some units
High-pressure fuel pump failure
Camshaft adjuster wear affecting timing
entries.cta: Get quotes for M270 Mercedes Benz CLA engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The M260 succeeded the M270 as Mercedes' 2.0 litre turbo petrol, debuting in the C118 CLA with revised injection architecture and higher output variants for CLA 35 AMG.
compatibleVariants: CLA 220, CLA 220 4MATIC, CLA 250, CLA 250 4MATIC, CLA 250 Shooting Brake, CLA 35 AMG, CLA 35 AMG Shooting Brake
commonFailures: Oil consumption due to piston ring wear
Crankshaft quality issues on early production units
VANOS (variable valve timing) system complexity leading to rattling
entries.cta: Get quotes for M260 Mercedes Benz CLA engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The M282 is a Renault-Nissan-Mercedes co-developed 1.3 litre four-cylinder petrol, featuring a cylinder deactivation system for fuel economy in CLA 180 and CLA 200 models.
compatibleVariants: CLA 180, CLA 180 Shooting Brake, CLA 200 (late production), CLA 200 Shooting Brake
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch in high-mileage units
Carbon build-up on intake valves due to direct injection
Cylinder deactivation system solenoid failures
entries.cta: Get quotes for M282 Mercedes Benz CLA engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The B48B20A is a BMW-sourced 2.0 litre petrol engine fitted to select CLA 200 models during the C118 production run, bringing BMW's modular engine architecture to Mercedes' line-up.
commonFailures: VANOS solenoid failure causing rattle and timing errors
Coolant loss from expansion tank cracks
Oil filter housing leaks
entries.cta: Get quotes for B48B20A Mercedes Benz CLA engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The M133 was the world's most powerful production four-cylinder petrol engine when launched, producing 381hp in CLA 45 AMG - a landmark for Mercedes-AMG downsizing.
compatibleVariants: CLA 45 AMG, CLA 45 AMG 4MATIC, CLA 45 AMG Shooting Brake
commonFailures: Turbo bearing failure (high boost pressure takes toll on bearings, typically after 60,000 miles)
Cylinder head cracking between valves under track use
High-pressure fuel pump failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for M133 Mercedes Benz CLA engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The M139 is a hand-built AMG engine producing up to 421hp - the world's most powerful turbocharged four-cylinder production engine - with an electrically actuated wastegate and reverse-flow layout.
compatibleVariants: CLA 45 S AMG, CLA 45 S AMG 4MATIC, CLA 45 S AMG Shooting Brake
commonFailures: Cylinder head warpage under sustained high-load operation
Crankcase pressure build-up causing oil leaks
Turbocharger wastegate actuator failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for M139 Mercedes Benz CLA engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3 Litre Petrol PHEV
entries.familyHeading: M282 + Electric Motor - 1.3 Litre Plug-in Hybrid (2020-2025)
entries.history: The CLA 250e pairs the M282 1.3 litre petrol engine with a 15.6 kWh battery and electric motor, offering Mercedes' first compact plug-in hybrid powertrain in the CLA range.
commonFailures: High-voltage battery degradation reducing electric range (8-10 years typical lifespan)
Electric coolant pump failure
Charging control unit faults
entries.cta: Get quotes for M282 hybrid Mercedes Benz CLA engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Mercedes Benz CLA Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mercedes Benz CLA, particularly the C117 generation, has established clear engine failure patterns that UK owners need to understand. The N47D20C diesel unit dominates replacement enquiries due to timing chain failures, while M270 petrol variants and M133 AMG engines have their own known weak points. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Below, we break down the most common CLA engine failures, what repair actually costs at a dealer versus a specialist, and when full replacement is the only sensible option.

problems.group: Mercedes Benz CLA Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz CLA Timing Chain Failure - Why N47 Diesel Engines Rattle and Fail
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz CLA 220d, CLA 220d Shooting Brake (2015-2019, N47D20C)

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides, and sprockets (gearbox removal required).
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-80,000 miles if done before chain stretch. Only viable if engine has not jumped timing.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit fully rebuilt engine with upgraded tensioner and chain components. Bench tested, 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: 100,000+ miles. The definitive fix when chain has slipped or engine has interference damage.

problems.recommendation: On a Mercedes Benz CLA 220d worth £4,000-£7,000, preventative chain replacement is almost always the better value if you catch the rattle early - typically £1,400-£1,900 at a specialist versus £2,200-£2,800 at a dealer. However, once the chain has stretched to the point of timing errors or no-start conditions, replacement is the only safe route. We see too many owners chasing cheap repairs after failure; it rarely ends well.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt N47D20C engine prices for your Mercedes Benz CLA

problems.group: Mercedes Benz CLA EGR Cooler Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz CLA EGR Cooler Failure - Coolant Loss and Diesel Engine Overheating
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz CLA 200d, CLA 220 CDI, CLA 220d (2013-2019, OM651 & N47D20C)

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace EGR cooler, flush cooling system, refill with fresh coolant, clear fault codes.
repairOptions.longevity: 50,000-70,000 miles. Effective if caught before engine damage occurs.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine after coolant contamination or hydro-lock damage. Includes new EGR cooler.
repairOptions.longevity: Full engine life. Required when coolant has caused bearing or cylinder damage.

problems.recommendation: If you spot low coolant warnings or smell coolant while driving, get the EGR cooler inspected immediately. Specialist replacement at £550-£800 is straightforward and prevents a £2,500-£3,500 engine replacement. Ignoring this on OM651 and N47D20C CLA diesels is one of the most common ways owners accidentally write off an otherwise healthy car.
problems.cta: Get quotes for OM651 or N47D20C EGR cooler and engine replacement

problems.group: Mercedes Benz CLA Oil Consumption & Piston Ring Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz CLA Oil Consumption & Piston Ring Failure - Petrol Engine Burning
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz CLA 180, CLA 200, CLA 250 (2019-2023, M260 & M282 petrol)

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full engine strip, replace piston rings, hone cylinders, new gaskets and bolts.
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-80,000 miles. Labour-intensive but retains original engine block.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit fully rebuilt engine with upgraded piston rings designed to prevent recurrence. Bench tested, 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: 100,000+ miles. Often only marginally more expensive than piston ring replacement - and the more reliable long-term solution.

problems.recommendation: On a CLA 250 burning a litre of oil every 1,000 miles, we typically recommend full rebuilt engine replacement rather than just piston rings. The labour to strip and rebuild is almost identical, but a factory-rebuilt unit includes all new bearings, seals, and timing components - and the upgraded rings that fix the design weakness. The price difference is often just £500-£1,000.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M260 and M282 engine prices for your Mercedes Benz CLA

problems.group: Mercedes Benz CLA VANOS System Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz CLA VANOS System Failure - B48 Petrol Engine Rattle and Timing Errors

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace VANOS solenoids, camshaft phasers, timing chain and guides as precaution.
repairOptions.longevity: 50,000-70,000 miles. Effective if caught at first rattle signs.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with revised VANOS components. Bench tested, 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: 100,000+ miles. Required if timing has jumped or valves have contacted pistons.

problems.recommendation: The VANOS rattle on a cold start is your warning - don't ignore it. Specialist solenoid and phaser replacement at £1,000-£1,500 resolves the issue if done promptly. However, if you've been driving with the rattle for months, the chain has almost certainly stretched, and we'd advise moving straight to a rebuilt engine with upgraded timing components.
problems.cta: Get quotes for B48B20A engine replacement for your Mercedes Benz CLA

problems.h4: Mercedes Benz CLA Turbocharger Failure - M133 AMG Performance Engine Weakness
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz CLA 45 AMG, CLA 45 AMG Shooting Brake (2015-2019, M133)

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and replace turbocharger with OEM-spec unit, new oil feed lines, fresh oil and filter.
repairOptions.longevity: 50,000-70,000 miles. Valid if bottom end and top end are healthy.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit fully rebuilt M133 engine with upgraded turbo bearings, new oil pump, and recalibrated wastegate.
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000-100,000 miles. Recommended when turbo debris has circulated through the engine.

problems.recommendation: On a CLA 45 AMG, we rarely recommend turbo-only replacement unless the failure was caught immediately. Turbo debris almost always contaminates the oil system, and the cost of a second failure justifies going straight to a fully rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo components. At £5,000-£7,500 from a specialist, it's expensive - but it's half what Mercedes main dealers charge for a new engine.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M133 AMG engine prices for your Mercedes Benz CLA

problems.h4: Mercedes Benz CLA HPFP Failure - High-Pressure Fuel Pump Collapse
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz CLA 180, CLA 200, CLA 250 (2013-2019, M270 petrol)

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace HPFP, all four injectors, fuel rail, high-pressure lines, and fuel filter.
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-80,000 miles. Essential if caught before engine damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine after fuel dilution has damaged bearings and cylinder walls. Bench tested, 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: 100,000+ miles. Required when metal debris has circulated beyond the fuel system.

problems.recommendation: HPFP failure is one of those repairs where trying to cut corners backfires. We've seen owners replace just the pump - only for debris in the rail to destroy the new pump within 500 miles. If the pump has failed, replace the entire high-pressure fuel system at the same time. Specialist cost of £1,600-£2,200 is steep, but ignoring contamination leads to a £2,800-£4,000 engine replacement.
problems.cta: Get quotes for M270 engine replacement for your Mercedes Benz CLA

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mercedes Benz CLA
finalCta.paragraph: Engine failure on a CLA doesn't have to mean the end of the car. Whether you're dealing with N47 timing chain rattle, M133 turbo failure, or M270 HPFP collapse, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mercedes Benz CLA specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components designed to prevent the original failure from recurring, backed by a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mercedes Benz CLA Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mercedes Benz CLA and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Mercedes Benz CLA Engine Types - Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every engine type for the Mercedes Benz CLA is available through our UK network, with typical price ranges based on your exact variant, engine code and budget.

types.title: Mercedes Benz CLA Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Dismantled, inspected and rebuilt to manufacturer specifications with worn parts replaced. Oem standards throughout.
types.frontDescription: Dismantled, inspected and rebuilt to manufacturer specifications with worn parts replaced. Oem standards throughout.
types.frontDisclaimer: Best balance of cost and long-term reliability
types.backDescription: A reconditioned CLA engine undergoes full strip-down, crack testing and genuine-spec component replacement across N47, OM654 and M260 units.
backBullets: Every engine is blueprinted to factory tolerances
Ideal for CLA 220d and CLA 45 AMG owners seeking longevity

types.title: Mercedes Benz CLA Rebuilt Engines
types.description: Comprehensive overhaul with new piston rings, bearings, seals and timing components. Not fully remanufactured but thoroughly refreshed.

12-month warranty as standard through our network

types.frontDisclaimer: Most budget-friendly option - higher risk tolerance required

types.frontDescription: Industrial-grade full rebuild to exceed original specifications. Every component replaced or machined to factory-fresh condition.

engineSizes.h2: Mercedes Benz CLA Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every factory displacement fitted to the Mercedes Benz CLA across C117 and C118 generations, listed with compatible engine codes, variants, power outputs and common failure notes.

groups.title: Mercedes Benz CLA Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLA 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5-litre OM607 diesel, sourced from Renault, powered entry-level CLA 180d models during the first-generation C117 production run (2013-2019). It prioritised fuel economy over refinement, returning over 70 mpg in real-world driving, but lacks the low-end torque of larger CLA diesel units. This engine is rare in the used market, as most CLA buyers opted for the 2.1-litre OM651.
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz CLA 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLA 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre diesel category spans three distinct engine families across CLA generations. Early C117 CLA 220d models used the BMW-sourced N47D20C, infamous for timing chain tensioner failure. The facelift C117 and early C118 switched to Mercedes' own OM654, a modular four-cylinder with stepped bowl combustion and reduced internal friction. This displacement became the backbone of the CLA diesel range, covering everything from economy-focused 200d to higher-output 220d variants.
commonFailurePoints: N47D20C - timing chain tensioner failure (60-100k miles); OM654 - oil cooler leaks
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz CLA 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLA 2.1L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.1-litre OM651 was Mercedes' high-volume four-cylinder diesel throughout the early 2010s, powering the pre-facelift C117 CLA 200d and CLA 220 CDI. It offered strong torque (350 Nm) and genuine 60+ mpg economy, making it a popular choice for high-mileage owners. This engine was phased out when the CLA received the 2015 facelift, replaced by the BMW-sourced N47D20C in 220d models.
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz CLA 2.1L diesel engines

groups.title: Mercedes Benz CLA Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLA 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.3-litre M282 is a Renault-Nissan-Mercedes co-developed four-cylinder petrol, debuting in the second-generation C118 CLA (2019 onwards). It features cylinder deactivation (which shuts down cylinders 2 and 3 under light load) and direct injection, delivering surprising refinement for a small-displacement unit. This engine powers entry-level CLA 180 and CLA 200 models, offering a balance of urban efficiency and adequate performance.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain stretch at higher mileage
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz CLA 1.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLA 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6-litre M270 petrol engine powered first-generation C117 CLA 200 models from 2013 to 2019. This turbocharged four-cylinder featured direct injection and CAMTRONIC variable valve lift, delivering responsive performance with reasonable economy. It was the volume petrol engine for the CLA during the C117 era, before being replaced by the M282 1.3-litre in the C118 generation.
commonFailurePoints: High-pressure fuel pump failure (metal shedding into fuel system)
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz CLA 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLA 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre petrol category covers the widest range of CLA performance, from standard CLA 220 and CLA 250 models to AMG variants. Three engine families appear here: the M270 (C117 CLA 250), the M260 (C118 CLA 220, CLA 250, CLA 35 AMG), and the BMW-sourced B48B20A (C118 CLA 200). The M133 and M139 AMG engines, producing up to 421 hp, represent the pinnacle of Mercedes four-cylinder petrol engineering in the CLA range.
commonFailurePoints: M270 - HPFP failure; M260 - oil consumption (piston rings); M133 - turbo bearing failure; M139 - cylinder head warpage; B48 - VANOS solenoid failure
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz CLA 2.0L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Mercedes Benz CLA Hybrid / Electrified Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLA 1.3L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The CLA 250e pairs the M282 1.3-litre turbo petrol engine with a 75 kW electric motor and 15.6 kWh lithium-ion battery, creating Mercedes' first compact plug-in hybrid in the CLA range. Launched in 2020 on the C118 generation, the 250e offers up to 40 miles of electric-only range and CO2 emissions below 40 g/km, making it a compelling choice for company car drivers. The hybrid system adds significant weight but delivers instant torque from a standstill. Not sure which engine size your Mercedes Benz CLA needs? Enter your registration number above - we’ll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Mercedes Benz CLA specialists.
items.cta: Enquire about Mercedes Benz CLA 1.3L hybrid engine availability

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLA Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel powered the majority of first-generation C117 CLA sales in the UK, with the CLA 220d becoming the most searched-for replacement engine due to timing chain failures on the BMW-sourced N47D20C unit.
items.descriptor: Diesel powered the majority of first-generation C117 CLA sales in the UK, with the CLA 220d becoming the most searched-for replacement engine due to timing chain failures on the BMW-sourced N47D20C unit.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mercedes Benz CLA Diesel Engine Families
families: OM651 - Mercedes 2.1L (CLA 200d, CLA 220 CDI), pre-facelift workhorse with head gasket weakness
N47D20C - BMW 2.0L (CLA 220d 2015-2019), notorious rear timing chain failure
OM654 - Mercedes 2.0L (CLA 200d/220d C118), modular design with improved efficiency
knownFor: Excellent motorway fuel economy (60+ mpg real-world)
N47D20C timing chain tensioner failure between 60k-100k miles
EGR cooler cracks causing coolant loss on OM651 and N47 units
High UK replacement demand on CLA 220d models

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLA Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol CLA engines span entry-level 1.3L units to the most powerful four-cylinder production engine in the world - the M139 in CLA 45 S, with replacement demand split between M270 HPFP failures and M260 oil consumption issues.
items.descriptor: Petrol CLA engines span entry-level 1.3L units to the most powerful four-cylinder production engine in the world - the M139 in CLA 45 S, with replacement demand split between M270 HPFP failures and M260 oil consumption issues.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mercedes Benz CLA Petrol Engine Families
families: M270 - 1.6L/2.0L (CLA 200, CLA 250 C117), high-pressure fuel pump weakness
M282 - 1.3L Renault co-developed (CLA 180/200 C118), cylinder deactivation
B48B20A - BMW-sourced 2.0L (CLA 200 C118), VANOS solenoid failures
M260 - 2.0L (CLA 220/250/35 AMG C118), piston ring oil consumption
M133 - 2.0L AMG (CLA 45 C117), turbo bearing failure under high boost
M139 - 2.0L AMG (CLA 45 S C118), hand-built, cylinder head warpage risk
knownFor: M270 HPFP metal shedding contaminating entire fuel system
M260 oil consumption due to piston ring wear (40k-70k miles)
M133/M139 extreme power density demands specialist rebuild expertise
CLA 45 AMG commands highest replacement margin in CLA range

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLA Plug-in Hybrid (PHEV) Engines

items.descriptor: The CLA 250e joined the range in 2020 as Mercedes' first compact plug-in hybrid, pairing the M282 1.3L petrol engine with a 75 kW electric motor for up to 40 miles of electric-only driving.
items.familiesLabel: Mercedes Benz CLA PHEV Engine Codes

engineYears.h2: Mercedes Benz CLA Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Mercedes Benz CLA production timeline from 2013 to the present, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: C117 Launch: M270 Petrol & OM651 Diesel Arrive
years.description: The first-generation CLA (C117) launched in 2013, introducing Mercedes' MFA platform with a range of four-cylinder petrol and diesel engines that would define the model's early replacement landscape.
keyChanges: Launch of four-door coupé body style (C117)
Petrol: M270 1.6L (CLA 180/200) and 2.0L (CLA 250)
Diesel: OM651 2.1L (CLA 200 CDI, CLA 220 CDI)
6-speed manual or 7-speed DCT transmission options
4MATIC all-wheel drive available on select variants
mainEngines: OM651 DE22 LA - 2.1L diesel (CLA 200 CDI, CLA 220 CDI)
M133 DE20 AL - 2.0L AMG petrol (CLA 45 AMG, from late 2013)
knownFor: CLA 220 CDI with OM651 is the most reliable diesel of the early generation
M270 petrol engines suffer from timing chain stretch and HPFP failure
Early CLA 45 AMG (360hp) set records as most powerful production four-cylinder
Rear timing chain on OM651 makes replacement labour-intensive
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013-2015 Mercedes Benz CLA replacement engines
years.ticker: Mercedes Benz CLA 220 CDI engine replacement • Mercedes Benz CLA 200 timing chain failure • OM651 diesel engine problems CLA
enquiries: Mercedes Benz CLA 220 CDI engine replacement
Mercedes Benz CLA 200 timing chain failure

years.preview: C117 Facelift: N47 Diesel & M133 Power Upgrade
years.description: The 2015 facelift brought significant engine changes, most notably the BMW-sourced N47D20C diesel in CLA 220d models - an engine now notorious for timing chain failures driving UK replacement demand.
keyChanges: CLA 220d replaces CLA 220 CDI with N47D20C 2.0L diesel (BMW-sourced)
CLA 45 AMG power increased from 360hp to 381hp
Shooting Brake (X117) estate body style introduced
CLA 180d launched with OM607 1.5L Renault diesel
CLA 250 Sport and 4MATIC variants added
mainEngines: M133 DE20 AL (facelift) - 2.0L AMG petrol, 381hp (CLA 45 AMG)
knownFor: N47D20C timing chain tensioner failure between 60k-100k miles - highest replacement demand in CLA range
OM607 Renault diesel offers excellent economy but injector seal failures
CLA 45 AMG (381hp) remains sought-after but turbo bearing failure is common
Shooting Brake variants generate separate long-tail estate queries
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015-2018 Mercedes Benz CLA replacement engines
years.ticker: Mercedes Benz CLA 220d engine replacement • N47D20C timing chain failure CLA • CLA 220d rattling noise on start • CLA 180d engine problems

years.preview: C118 Launch: M282 Petrol, OM654 Diesel & AMG M139
years.description: The second-generation CLA (C118) launched on the new MFA2 platform, introducing Mercedes' Renault-co-developed M282 1.3L petrol, the advanced OM654 2.0L diesel, and the record-breaking M139 AMG engine.
keyChanges: New MFA2 platform with longer wheelbase and wider track
Petrol: M282 1.3L (CLA 180/200) replaces M270 1.6L
Diesel: OM654 2.0L (CLA 200d/220d) introduces aluminium block construction
CLA 35 AMG (306hp) joins range with M260 engine
CLA 45 S AMG (421hp) debuts with hand-built M139 engine
MBUX infotainment system introduced

M139 DE20 LA - 2.0L AMG petrol, 421hp (CLA 45 S AMG)
knownFor: OM654 diesel is Mercedes' most advanced four-cylinder - oil cooler leaks reported
M282 petrol suffers from carbon build-up on intake valves
M139 is world's most powerful four-cylinder production engine (421hp)
M260 petrol known for oil consumption due to piston ring wear
48V mild hybrid (EQ Boost) on select variants from 2020

48V mild hybrid (EQ Boost) added to 2.0L petrol variants

faq.h2: Mercedes Benz CLA Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mercedes Benz CLA engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A Mercedes Benz CLA replacement engine typically costs between £1,500 for a used unit and £4,500 for a rebuilt diesel, with AMG variants reaching £11,000. Specialist fitting adds £450-£800.
keyPoints: Used CLA engines: £900-£2,200 depending on mileage and engine code
Rebuilt OM654/N47 diesel units: £2,000-£4,200 supply only
M133/M139 AMG rebuilt engines: £4,500-£11,000
Specialist fitting adds £450-£800; dealer labour significantly higher
12-month warranty included on all rebuilt units through EngineMarket
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz CLA engine replacement quotes from UK specialists today

items.question: Is the Mercedes Benz CLA 220d engine reliable? What problems should I watch for?
items.answer: The CLA 220d uses two different engines. Pre-2019 N47D20C (BMW-sourced) has well-documented timing chain tensioner failure between 60k-100k miles. Post-2019 OM654 is more reliable but has NOx sensor and cold start issues.
keyPoints: N47D20C timing chain rattling on cold start signals imminent tensioner failure
OM654 (2019+) owners report rough idle/shaking for 30 seconds on cold starts - Mercedes calls this "normal characteristic"
NOx sensor failures common on OM654, requiring cleaning or replacement every 10k-15k miles
EGR cooler cracks affect both engine families, causing coolant loss
220d is most searched CLA diesel for replacement due to N47 timing chain reputation
items.cta: Get a specialist inspection for your CLA 220d before it fails

items.question: How long do Mercedes Benz CLA engines last?
items.answer: Well-maintained CLA diesel engines (OM651/OM654) regularly reach 150,000-200,000 miles. Petrol M270/M260 units typically last 120,000-150,000 miles. N47D20C diesels often fail earlier due to timing chain tensioner weakness.
keyPoints: OM651 2.1L diesel (2013-2015): 180k+ miles with regular servicing
OM654 2.0L diesel (2019+): too new for full data but promising design
N47D20C (2015-2019): timing chain often fails 60k-100k miles - preventative replacement extends life
M270 petrol: 120k-150k miles; HPFP failure common after 80k
Regular oil changes every 10k miles (not Mercedes' 15k-18k interval) significantly extends life
items.cta: Find a reconditioned CLA engine with extended warranty coverage

items.question: Which Mercedes Benz CLA engine should I avoid when buying used?
items.answer: The 2015-2019 CLA 220d with the N47D20C engine carries the highest risk due to timing chain tensioner failure. Early 2013-2015 CLA 250 petrol units also show higher complaint rates for HPFP and timing issues.
keyPoints: N47D20C (CLA 220d 2015-2019) is most discussed for catastrophic chain failure
2014 CLA 250 has more owner complaints than any other year, including blown engine reports
M270 petrol HPFP failure sends metal debris through fuel system - costly repair
OM607 1.5L diesel (CLA 180d) is underpowered but mechanically robust
Post-2019 OM654 and M282 engines show fewer major failures but have NOx sensor annoyances
items.cta: Check CLA engine reliability ratings before you buy - get our buying guide

items.question: How much does timing chain replacement cost on a Mercedes Benz CLA?
items.answer: Timing chain replacement on a CLA costs £1,400-£1,900 at a specialist or £2,200-£2,800 at a main dealer. This is preventative work. If the chain has already slipped, full engine replacement at £2,800-£3,800 is required.
keyPoints: Specialist preventative chain/tensioner replacement: £1,400-£1,900 including gearbox removal
Dealer pricing for same work: £2,200-£2,800
Labour-intensive because chain is at rear of engine on most CLA diesels
Rattling noise on cold start for 1-2 seconds is the early warning sign
Once timing jumps, valves contact pistons - engine is beyond repair
items.cta: Get quotes for N47D20C timing chain replacement before it fails

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Mercedes Benz CLA?
items.answer: Yes, if the car is otherwise sound. A CLA worth £4,000-£7,000 with a failed N47 diesel justifies a £2,800-£3,800 rebuilt engine. For older, high-mileage petrol models, used engine at £1,200-£2,200 is often the smarter financial choice.
keyPoints: CLA 220d with good body/interior: replacement engine is cost-effective
CLA 180 or 200 petrol with 120k+ miles: consider used engine over rebuilt
AMG CLA 45: always worth rebuilding - cars hold strong value
Specialist rebuilt engines fix original design weaknesses and include warranty
Compare against £4,000-£5,000 main dealer new engine quotes
items.cta: See if your CLA qualifies for cost-effective engine replacement

items.question: What are the most common Mercedes Benz CLA engine problems by year?
items.answer: 2013-2015 models suffer OM651 chain stretch and M270 HPFP failure. 2015-2019 CLA 220d N47D20C is worst for timing chain tensioner failure. 2019+ models have OM654 cold start misfire/shaking and NOx sensor faults, plus M260 oil consumption.
keyPoints: 2013-2015 (OM651 diesel): timing chain can stretch - listen for metallic clatter
2014 (CLA 250 petrol): highest complaint volume including engine failure
2015-2019 (N47D20C diesel): timing chain tensioner failure 60k-100k miles
2019-2023 (OM654 diesel): cold start shaking/misfire for 30 seconds - dealer says "normal"
2019-2023 (M260 petrol): piston ring wear causing oil consumption
items.cta: Match your CLA's year to common problems - compare specialist repair costs

items.question: Can I fit a used engine to my Mercedes Benz CLA?
items.answer: Yes, used CLA engines are a budget-friendly option at £900-£2,200. Ensure the donor engine has the exact same engine code (e.g., N47D20C not N47D20) and comes from the same generation C117 or C118.
keyPoints: Used engines are 50-70% cheaper than rebuilt units
Risk: unknown service history and hidden wear
Only buy from breakers offering compression test results
Match engine code exactly - M270 has 1.6L and 2.0L variants
30-90 day warranty typical; less protection than 12 months on rebuilt
items.cta: Check used CLA engine availability from UK breakers

items.question: Does a rebuilt Mercedes Benz CLA engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and workmanship. Premium suppliers offer 24-36 month warranties on remanufactured units.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage is standard on all rebuilt CLA engines
Remanufactured engines often carry 24-36 month coverage
Warranty covers internal component failure, not external ancillaries
Supply & fit packages include labour warranty through fitting specialist
Always confirm exact terms - some warranties exclude timing chain (but ours don't)
items.cta: Compare warrantied CLA engine prices from trusted UK specialists

items.question: Mercedes Benz CLA petrol vs diesel - which engine is more reliable?
items.answer: Diesel OM651 (2013-2015) is most robust but older. OM654 diesel has cold start quirks but no major failures. Petrol M270 suffers HPFP failure; M260 has oil consumption. Neither fuel type is clearly more reliable - condition and maintenance matter more.
keyPoints: Pre-2015 OM651 diesel: chain stretch possible but otherwise solid
N47D20C diesel (2015-2019): timing chain risk - avoid without proof of replacement
OM654 diesel (2019+): NOx sensor issues, cold start shaking - not mechanical failure
M270 petrol: HPFP failure contaminates entire fuel system
For high mileage (15k+ annual), diesel economy wins; for short journeys, petrol avoids DPF issues
items.cta: Get quotes for both petrol and diesel CLA engines - compare prices
